mission.title: Install and Configure Knative Eventing (Knative) on Kubernetes
mission.description: Knative Eventing is an event-driven application platform for Kubernetes that enables secure event processing and discovery using CloudEvents. You would install it to build applications that respond to events in a scalable and efficient manner.

steps.title: Install Knative Eventing CRDs
steps.description:
Install the Knative Eventing CRDs from the official release:
```bash
kubectl apply -f https://github.com/knative/eventing/releases/download/knative-v1.17.5/eventing-crds.yaml
```

steps.title: Install Knative Eventing core
steps.description:
Install the core eventing components:
```bash
kubectl apply -f https://github.com/knative/eventing/releases/download/knative-v1.17.5/eventing-core.yaml
```

steps.title: Install the in-memory channel (for development)
steps.description:
Install the in-memory channel for development/testing:
```bash
kubectl apply -f https://github.com/knative/eventing/releases/download/knative-v1.17.5/in-memory-channel.yaml
```
For production, use Apache Kafka or NATS channels instead.

steps.title: Install the MT-Channel-Based broker
steps.description:
Install the default broker implementation:
```bash
kubectl apply -f https://github.com/knative/eventing/releases/download/knative-v1.17.5/mt-channel-broker.yaml
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check all eventing pods are running:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n knative-eventing
kubectl get apiservices | grep knative
```

uninstall.title: Remove Knative Eventing components
uninstall.description:
```bash
kubectl delete -f https://github.com/knative/eventing/releases/download/knative-v1.17.5/mt-channel-broker.yaml
kubectl delete -f https://github.com/knative/eventing/releases/download/knative-v1.17.5/in-memory-channel.yaml
kubectl delete -f https://github.com/knative/eventing/releases/download/knative-v1.17.5/eventing-core.yaml
kubectl delete -f https://github.com/knative/eventing/releases/download/knative-v1.17.5/eventing-crds.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Apply the new version manifests
upgrade.description:
Check https://github.com/knative/eventing/releases for the latest version:
```bash
kubectl apply -f https://github.com/knative/eventing/releases/download/knative-v1.17.5/eventing-crds.yaml
kubectl apply -f https://github.com/knative/eventing/releases/download/knative-v1.17.5/eventing-core.yaml
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in Pending
troubleshooting.description:
Check resource availability:
```bash
kubectl describe pods -n knative-eventing
kubectl get events -n knative-eventing
```

troubleshooting.title: Webhook errors
troubleshooting.description:
Ensure the webhook service is running:
```bash
kubectl get svc -n knative-eventing
kubectl logs -l app=eventing-webhook -n knative-eventing
```

troubleshooting.title: Broker not creating channels
troubleshooting.description:
Verify the default channel configuration:
```bash
kubectl get configmap config-br-defaults -n knative-eventing -o yaml
```

resolution.summary: Knative Eventing is installed with the in-memory channel and MT-Channel-Based broker for event-driven architectures.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running version 1.24 or higher and have Helm and kubectl installed.
